records.ety: [F. exposer; pref. ex- (L. ex out)+poser to place. See Pose, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exposed(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Exposing.]

defs: To set forth; to set out to public view; to exhibit; to show; to display; as, to expose goods for sale; to expose pictures to public inspection.
quotes: Those who seek truth only, freely expose their principles to the test, and are pleased to have them examined.

defs: To lay bare; to lay open to attack, danger, or anything objectionable; to render accessible to anything which may affect, especially detrimentally; to make liable; as, to expose one's self to the heat of the sun, or to cold, insult, danger, or ridicule; to expose an army to destruction or defeat.
quotes: Expose thyself to feel what wretches feel.

defs: To deprive of concealment; to discover; to lay open to public inspection, or bring to public notice, as a thing that shuns publicity, something criminal, shameful, or the like; as, to expose the faults of a neighbor.
quotes: You only expose the follies of men, without arraigning their vices.

defs: To disclose the faults or reprehensible practices of; to lay open to general condemnation or contempt by making public the character or arts of; as, to expose a cheat, liar, or hypocrite.
